title: Mandatory Military Service
description: All able-bodied citizens are required to complete a term of military service. Troop numbers swell; civil liberties contract.

approveEffect.title: Conscription Drive
approveEffect.description: The draft notice goes out. Every household sends someone.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Every generation must learn that liberty is defended, not inherited.

tr_populist.flavor: The poor march to war. The wealthy find reasons to stay home.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Conscript armies are statistically less effective than professional ones per unit cost.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Disciplined workers make disciplined soldiers. I see no downside.

title: Peace Dividend
description: Redirect military budget to civil infrastructure. Popular with progressives; alarming to security hawks.

approveEffect.title: Peace Dividend
approveEffect.description: Swords into plowshares — and plowshares into coin.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: A nation that dismantles its sword will soon need to borrow one.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Funds redirected from guns to roads and clinics. A start, at least.

tr_technocrat.flavor: The fiscal reallocation projections are genuinely favorable here.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Defense contracts are profitable. This is not the direction I'd choose.

title: Green Energy Transition
description: Phase out fossil fuels and invest in renewable energy infrastructure. Expensive in the short term; ecologically transformative.

approveEffect.title: Transition Cost
approveEffect.description: The transition is costly. The old infrastructure doesn't go quietly.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: The cost of doing nothing has always exceeded the cost of acting.

tr_industrialist.flavor: You're asking me to dismantle the engine of civilization on a promise.

tr_technocrat.flavor: The infrastructure investment has solid long-term efficiency projections.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Energy independence is a strategic asset. I'll allow it.

title: State Religious Patronage
description: Formally fund and endorse a state religious institution. Deeply meaningful to the faithful; alienating to secularists.

approveEffect.title: Temple Upkeep
approveEffect.description: The temples must be maintained. The state will see to it.

tr_populist.flavor: Faith is the one thing that has always been free. Don't put a price on it.

tr_technocrat.flavor: State religion introduces irrational variables into governance modeling.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Many traditions carry deep ecological wisdom. I won't oppose this outright.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: A people united in faith are harder to divide. There is strategic value here.

title: Secular Education Act
description: Standardize public education around civic and scientific curricula, removing religious instruction from state schools.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Standardized empirical education is the single highest-return investment a state can make.

tr_populist.flavor: You are asking parents to trust the state more than their own faith. Tread carefully.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Scientific literacy and environmental stewardship are deeply linked. Yes.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: A technically educated populace produces better engineers and officers. Fine.

title: Emergency Conscription
description: A rapid but brutal expansion of the military through forced enlistment. High troop yield; severe civil cost.

approveEffect.title: Emergency Conscription
approveEffect.description: No volunteers needed. You're all volunteering.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Desperate times. I will not pretend this is anything other than what it is.

tr_populist.flavor: Chains don't stop being chains just because you call them uniforms.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Short-term troop surge with severe long-term morale degradation. Use sparingly.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Workers pulled from the factories cost me more than the troops are worth.

title: Progressive Tax Reform
description: Restructure the tax code to place greater burden on accumulated wealth. Popular with the poor; despised by elites.

approveEffect.title: Progressive Taxation
approveEffect.description: The wealthy discover that the law applies to them too.

tr_industrialist.flavor: You are describing the redistribution of ambition.

tr_populist.flavor: The people didn't ask for much. Just their fair share.

tr_technocrat.flavor: The revenue modeling on this is solid. Behavioral tax elasticity may reduce yield slightly.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Wealth concentrated in few hands rarely finds its way to conservation.

title: Austerity Measures
description: Slash public spending to stabilize national finances. Improves the budget dramatically; popular with no one.

approveEffect.title: Austerity Measures
approveEffect.description: The belt tightens. The treasury breathes.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Fiscal discipline is the foundation of national strength. Painful but necessary.

tr_populist.flavor: Tell me which services you're cutting and I'll tell you whose children pay for it.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Short-term stability gain. Long-term infrastructure deficit. Acceptable if temporary.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Conservation budgets are never considered essential until they're gone.

title: Free Press Protection Act
description: Enshrine press freedom and prohibit state censorship. Liberties expand; state control narrows.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Information flow is a civic infrastructure. This protects it.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: The enemy reads our press too. Freedom of speech has a cost in wartime.

tr_populist.flavor: A free press is the only mirror powerful people are afraid to look into.

tr_industrialist.flavor: A free press also investigates my books. I have reservations.

title: Border Fortification Program
description: Construct permanent defensive installations along national borders. Expensive and ecologically disruptive; strategically reassuring.

approveEffect.title: Fortification Cost
approveEffect.description: Stone and steel where the border used to be a suggestion.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: A wall is not a strategy, but it is a very good start.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Every kilometer of wall is a migration corridor destroyed.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Construction contracts. I know several excellent firms.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Static defenses have poor cost-efficiency versus mobile deterrence. But they reassure citizens, which has value.

title: Civic Works Programme
description: A major government-led investment in roads, housing, and public utilities. Costly but broadly beloved.

approveEffect.title: Civic Works
approveEffect.description: Roads. Bridges. Housing. The state, building.

tr_populist.flavor: Roads, homes, water. These are the things people actually need.

tr_technocrat.flavor: The multiplier effects of public infrastructure investment are well-documented.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Government-built roads are roads I didn't have to build. I'll take it.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: It depends entirely on where they're building and what they're paving over.

title: National Surveillance Programme
description: Deploy widespread monitoring of communications and public spaces in the name of security. Effective; deeply unpopular with libertarians.

approveEffect.title: Surveillance Network
approveEffect.description: The cameras never blink. Neither does the state.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Intelligence is force multiplier. The discomfort of the innocent is a worthwhile cost.

tr_technocrat.flavor: The data security implications alone make this difficult to recommend without significant oversight.

tr_populist.flavor: They call it safety. We call it being watched.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: A state that watches its citizens this closely will eventually watch dissidents too.

title: Subsidize Coal Industry
description: Redirect government funds to domestic coal producers, boosting energy output and industrial jobs.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Burying coal ash in our children's future is not an energy policy.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Domestic fuel keeps the war machine turning when supply lines fail.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Coal built this civilization; withdrawing its support now is the height of ingratitude.

tr_populist.flavor: It's the miners' lungs that pay the real subsidy — not the shareholders'.

title: Expand Standing Army
description: Authorize a major increase in military personnel and equipment. Costs are significant; so is the deterrent effect.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Hesitation on the border is an invitation written in blood.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: Every cannon forged is a forest that will not be planted.

tr_populist.flavor: The common soldier's family does not share in the general's glory.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Secure borders are good for business — the tariff lines practically write themselves.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Projection of force has measurable deterrence value when calibrated correctly.

title: Civil Liberties Reform
description: Expand rights of assembly, speech, and religious practice. Popular with diverse populations; seen as weakness by traditionalists.

tr_environmentalist.flavor: A free people are far more likely to protect what they love — including the land.

tr_military_hawk.flavor: Freedoms are luxuries that vanish the moment the enemy arrives at the gate.

tr_technocrat.flavor: Protected civil discourse tends to produce more accurate and actionable civic data.

tr_populist.flavor: Rights aren't privileges handed down from above — they belong to every person who breathes.

tr_industrialist.flavor: Too much freedom in the streets means not enough order where it counts.
